goal: Build a local screenshot check that opens Browser Preview HTML in a real headless browser, writes a PNG screenshot, validates it, and emits JSON evidence without claiming human review.
claims: tools/browser-screenshot-check.cjs launches an available local Chromium-family browser in headless mode.
The tool writes a PNG screenshot to a caller-provided path.
The tool validates PNG signature and minimum size.
The output states browser_automation=true, screenshot_check=true, and human_ui_review=false.
Fixture tests cover argument generation, PNG validation, no-browser behavior, and real screenshot flow when a browser is available.
assumptions: A local Edge or Chrome binary is acceptable for the first screenshot check.
Screenshot artifacts are generated in temp or caller-controlled paths and are not committed.
Human UI Review remains a separate BP-C4 task.
dod: A local pipeline smoke generates and validates a PNG screenshot when a browser is available.
stop_conditions: Human UI Review claim becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-105 added real browser automation but explicitly did not claim screenshot or human UI review evidence.
